title: Barbara Frietchie
content:
В В В В Up from the meadows rich with corn,
В В В В Clear in the cool September morn,

В В В В The clustered spires of Frederick stand
В В В В Green-walled by the hills of Maryland.

В В В В Round about them orchards sweep,
В В В В Apple and peach tree fruited deep,

В В В В Fair as the garden of the Lord
В В В В To the eyes of the famished rebel horde,

В В В В On that pleasant morn of the early fall
В В В В When Lee marched over the mountain-wall,

В В В В Over the mountains winding down,
В В В В Horse and foot, into Frederick town.

В В В В Forty flags with their silver stars,
В В В В Forty flags with their crimson bars,

В В В В Flapped in the morning wind: the sun
В В В В Of noon looked down, and saw not one.

В В В В Up rose old Barbara Frietchie then,
В В В В Bowed with her fourscore years and ten;

В В В В Bravest of all in Frederick town,
В В В В She took up the flag the men hauled down;

В В В В In her attic window the staff she set,
В В В В To show that one heart was loyal yet.

В В В В Up the street came the rebel tread,
В В В В Stonewall Jackson riding ahead.

В В В В Under his slouched hat left and right
В В В В He glanced; the old flag met his sight.

В В В В пїЅHalt!пїЅ the dust-brown ranks stood fast.
В В В В пїЅFire!пїЅ out blazed the rifle-blast.

В В В В It shivered the window, pane and sash;
В В В В It rent the banner with seam and gash.

В В В В Quick, as it fell, from the broken staff
В В В В Dame Barbara snatched the silken scarf.

В В В В She leaned far out on the window-sill,
В В В В And shook it forth with a royal will.

В В В В пїЅShoot, if you must, this old gray head,
В В В В But spare your countryпїЅs flag,пїЅ she said.

В В В В A shade of sadness, a blush of shame,
В В В В Over the face of the leader came;

В В В В The nobler nature within him stirred
В В В В To life at that womanпїЅs deed and word;

В В В В пїЅWho touches a hair of yon gray head
В В В В Dies like a dog! March on!пїЅ he said.

В В В В All day long through Frederick street
В В В В Sounded the tread of marching feet:

В В В В All day long that free flag tost
В В В В Over the heads of the rebel host.

В В В В Ever its torn folds rose and fell
В В В В On the loyal winds that loved it well;

В В В В And through the hill-gaps sunset light
В В В В Shone over it with a warm good-night.

В В В В Barbara FrietchieпїЅs work is oпїЅer,
В В В В And the Rebel rides on his raids no more.

В В В В Honor to her! and let a tear
В В В В Fall, for her sake, on StonewallпїЅs bier.

В В В В Over Barbara FrietchieпїЅs grave,
В В В В Flag of Freedom and Union, wave!

В В В В Peace and order and beauty draw
В В В В Round thy symbol of light and law;

В В В В And ever the stars above look down
В В В В On thy stars below in Frederick town!
meaning:
John Greenleaf Whittier's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Barbara Frietchie"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
